growth_records.strain: ZJU2 (DSM2343-derived, GOX1231 and GOX1081 deleted; reference/parent strain)
growth_records.medium: Glucose fermentation medium (corn steep liquor based)

up.method: bio-analyzer (SBA-40D); glucose rapidly oxidized and exhausted at 30 h

sec.method: HPLC; ~130 g/L GA accumulated by 30 h when glucose exhausted

growth_records.cite: Yuan J et al. Combinatorial metabolic engineering of industrial Gluconobacter oxydans DSM2343 for boosting 5-keto-D-gluconic acid accumulation. BMC Biotechnology (2016). DOI:10.1186/s12896-016-0272-y. [PMC4869267]
growth_records.snippet: The reference strain, G. oxydans ZJU2, accumulated 82.48 +/- 1.10 g/L 5-KGA in the broth, but the residual GA was quite high at about 62.07 +/- 1.04 g/L... The glucose was rapidly oxidized and exhausted at 30 h, while about 130 +/- 1.76 g/L
growth_records.notes: Parent strain batch, 15 L, DOT-rich, 30C, pH controlled at 5.0 with 5 M NaOH. Products measured by HPLC; 5-KGA 82.48 g/L, residual GA 62.07 g/L. Max DCW 3.58 g/L (from Table 2). Specific growth rate not reported. Glucose 100-150 g/L (exact per-strain initial not pinned); glucose exhausted at 30 h. Cell yield of G. oxydans cited from literature ~0.09 g cdw/g glucose (refs 28,29), NOT measured here.

growth_records.medium: Glucose fermentation medium (corn steep liquor based)

up.method: maximal OTR at 19 h (volumetric); off-gas analysis

sec.method: maximal CTR at 32 h (volumetric); off-gas analysis

growth_records.cite: Yuan J et al. Combinatorial metabolic engineering of industrial Gluconobacter oxydans DSM2343 for boosting 5-keto-D-gluconic acid accumulation. BMC Biotechnology (2016). DOI:10.1186/s12896-016-0272-y. [PMC4869267]
growth_records.snippet: G. oxydans ZJU4 and G. oxydans ZJU5 reached their maximal OTR of 26.52 mmol/L h and 29.85 mmol/L h, respectively at 19 h... The specific maximal CO2 production rate (CTR) of both strains was 7.5 mmol/L h at 32 h.
growth_records.notes: PQQ-cluster overexpression strain, batch 15 L, excess O2, 30C, pH5.0. Measured exchange rates: max OTR 26.52 mmol/L/h @19 h; max CTR 7.5 mmol/L/h @32 h; after glucose consumption OTR fell to 1.5-1.8 mmol/L/h. 5-KGA 131.76 g/L, DCW 3.41 g/L, PQQ 674.82 ug/L (Table 2). OTR/CTR are volumetric (per L broth), not per gDW; DCW at time of max OTR not stated so specific qO2/qCO2 not computed.

growth_records.medium: Glucose fermentation medium (corn steep liquor based)

up.method: maximal OTR at 19 h (volumetric); off-gas analysis

growth_records.cite: Yuan J et al. Combinatorial metabolic engineering of industrial Gluconobacter oxydans DSM2343 for boosting 5-keto-D-gluconic acid accumulation. BMC Biotechnology (2016). DOI:10.1186/s12896-016-0272-y. [PMC4869267]
growth_records.snippet: G. oxydans ZJU4 and G. oxydans ZJU5 reached their maximal OTR of 26.52 mmol/L h and 29.85 mmol/L h, respectively at 19 h... The CTR decreased below 5.0 mmol/L h at 57 h for the G. oxydans ZJU5 strain.
growth_records.notes: PQQ-cluster + tldD overexpression, batch 15 L, excess O2, 30C, pH5.0. Max OTR 29.85 mmol/L/h @19 h; max CTR 7.5 mmol/L/h @32 h. 5-KGA 134.88 g/L, DCW 3.40 g/L, PQQ 757.83 ug/L (Table 2). Volumetric rates only.

growth_records.medium: Glucose fermentation medium (corn steep liquor based)
growth_records.cite: Yuan J et al. Combinatorial metabolic engineering of industrial Gluconobacter oxydans DSM2343 for boosting 5-keto-D-gluconic acid accumulation. BMC Biotechnology (2016). DOI:10.1186/s12896-016-0272-y. [PMC4869267]
growth_records.snippet: The maximal OTR of 35.83 mmol/L h and 38.97 mmol/L h were achieved at 11 h, respectively. After 22 h, the CTRs of ZJU6 and ZJU7 reached the maximal values of 8.38 mmol/L h and 8.98 mmol/L h... 141.86 +/- 2.89 g/L and 144.52 +/- 2.94 g/L 5-K
growth_records.notes: cytochrome bo3 oxidase engineered strain, batch 15 L, excess O2, 30C, pH5.0. Max OTR 35.83 mmol/L/h @11 h; max CTR 8.38 mmol/L/h @22 h. 5-KGA 141.86 g/L. H+/O ratio 1.95, ubiquinol oxidase activity 0.78 umol/min/mg (Table 3). Volumetric rates only.

growth_records.medium: Glucose fermentation medium (corn steep liquor based)

up.method: bio-analyzer; glucose oxidation accelerated, exhausted at 20 h

sec.method: maximal CTR at 22 h (volumetric); fell to 0.5 mmol/L/h at 47 h

growth_records.cite: Yuan J et al. Combinatorial metabolic engineering of industrial Gluconobacter oxydans DSM2343 for boosting 5-keto-D-gluconic acid accumulation. BMC Biotechnology (2016). DOI:10.1186/s12896-016-0272-y. [PMC4869267]
growth_records.snippet: the titer and productivity of 5-KGA reached 144.52 +/- 2.94 g/L and 2.26 g/(L h), respectively, in a 15 L fermenter... The glucose oxidation rate was accelerated, and glucose was exhausted at 20 h. The maximal OTR of ... 38.97 mmol/L h ...
growth_records.notes: Best batch strain (all engineering combined), 15 L, excess O2, 30C, pH5.0. Max OTR 38.97 mmol/L/h @11 h; max CTR 8.98 mmol/L/h @22 h (to 0.5 by 47 h). 5-KGA 144.52 g/L, productivity 2.26 g/(L.h). Glucose exhausted at 20 h. H+/O ratio 2.01, ubiquinol oxidase 0.80 umol/min/mg. Volumetric exchange rates only.

growth_records.cite: Yuan J et al. Combinatorial metabolic engineering of industrial Gluconobacter oxydans DSM2343 for boosting 5-keto-D-gluconic acid accumulation. BMC Biotechnology (2016). DOI:10.1186/s12896-016-0272-y. [PMC4869267]
growth_records.snippet: It could be further improved the 5-KGA titer by 12.10 % through the fed-batch fermentation under the pH shift and dissolved oxygen tension (DOT) control condition, obtained 162 +/- 2.12 g/L with the productivity of 2.53 g/(L h) within 64 h.
growth_records.notes: Fed-batch, two-stage pH control (shift to 4.5 for 5-KGA phase), DOT control, 30C. 5-KGA 162 g/L, productivity 2.53 g/(L.h) within 64 h (matches 162/64=2.53). Initial 6 L, 100 g/L glucose; 2 L feed of 1400 g glucose added at 30 g/L, maintained 30-40 g/L. Presumed best strain (ZJU7). No growth rate reported.
